title: ageratina cupressorum
intro: Ageratina cupressorum, also known as Mexican tea or tree daisy, is a plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Some of the health benefits associated with Ageratina cupressorum include its ability to improve digestion, boost the immune system, and reduce stress levels. Ageratina cupressorum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
